message:
feat(server): heap-scheduled ATC review loop, exact-expiry force-release, performance instrumentation

Replace the linear scan of all agents on every ATC tick with a
BinaryHeap-based priority queue (ScheduledAgentReview).  Each agent
carries a monotonic schedule_version; stale heap entries are
suppressed on pop, so rescheduling is O(log N) without heap removal.

Key changes in atc.rs:
- Add elapsed_micros() helper and const fn state_count().
- AgentLivenessEntry gains schedule_version + next_review_micros fields.
- New run_scheduled_reviews() drives the review loop from the heap,
  calling force_release_reservation with the Exact expiry constraint
  to avoid racing with concurrent renewals.
- Sweep-based fallback (sweep_and_schedule_all) rebuilds the heap
  periodically to recover from any drift.
- Emit structured timing metrics (review_agent_elapsed_us,
  sweep_elapsed_us) for observability.
- Extensive new unit tests for heap scheduling, stale-version
  suppression, and force-release integration.

In tui_app.rs: propagate the new AgentLivenessEntry fields through
the TUI rendering path.

In tui_bridge.rs: minor adjustment to match updated ATC API.

In tui_web_dashboard.rs: align the web dashboard JSON serialization
with the new schedule_version / next_review_micros fields and add
timing instrumentation for dashboard render latency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
